agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific int

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic complementarity while being tolerated by SSTR2. These changes should increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) by improving SSTR2 binding and reducing off-target affinity.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis is that increasing selectivity requires targeted disrupti

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that increasing selectivity requires targeted disruption of conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. This hypothesis is supported by the observed decline in Δmargin and the need to reduce off-target binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: Focus on non-pharmacophore positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
Propose mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These mutations are expected to reduce off-target binding while maintaining SSTR2 binding.
